payload.query: Discuss two or three recent post-1990 works that are likely to become true classics in the American politics literature. What makes each one a classic? What research question does each address and what void does each fill? What scholarship has flowed from each? How has each informed our understanding of American politics?

articles.title: Norm Macdonald Had His Own Kind of Honesty
articles.abstract: He became a comedian’s comedian by bucking the conventions of our confessional age.
articles.snippet: He became a comedian’s comedian by bucking the conventions of our confessional age.

articles.title: Cloris Leachman’s Golden Age Was Middle Age
articles.abstract: She was an actor for almost 80 years, winning Emmys into her 70s.
articles.snippet: She was an actor for almost 80 years, winning Emmys into her 70s.

articles.title: James Hormel Used His Spam Fortune to Cement a Place in Gay History
articles.abstract: He gave more than $15 million to L.G.B.T.Q. causes over his life and was appointed ambassador to Luxembourg, which caused a Senate standoff.
articles.snippet: He gave more than $15 million to L.G.B.T.Q. causes over his life and was appointed ambassador to Luxembourg, which caused a Senate standoff.

articles.title: Willie Garson Found a Home Playing the Canonical ‘Gay Best Friend’
articles.abstract: In a media climate when gay characters were usually flat, he found a playful middle ground.
articles.snippet: In a media climate when gay characters were usually flat, he found a playful middle ground.

articles.title: The Orphaned Gorilla That Touched the World
articles.abstract: A life in three viral photos.
articles.snippet: A life in three viral photos.

articles.title: Rosalind Cartwright Wanted to Understand Divorcing Women’s Dreams
articles.abstract: The women who arrived at Rosalind Cartwright’s sleep laboratory in Chicago in 1978, carrying toothbrushes and pajamas, were in pain.
articles.snippet: The women who arrived at Rosalind Cartwright’s sleep laboratory in Chicago in 1978, carrying toothbrushes and pajamas, were in pain.

articles.title: Rennie Davis, One of the Chicago Seven, Traded Activism for Inner Peace
articles.abstract: When he stopped trying to fix the world, it left some leftists feeling pretty irritated.
articles.snippet: When he stopped trying to fix the world, it left some leftists feeling pretty irritated.

articles.title: Mudcat Grant Sang Out Against Racism on the Baseball Field
articles.abstract: Fifty-six years before Colin Kaepernick took a knee and became a sensation, he mounted his own protest during the national anthem.
articles.snippet: Fifty-six years before Colin Kaepernick took a knee and became a sensation, he mounted his own protest during the national anthem.

articles.title: Did Larry King’s Obsession With Death Fuel His ‘Indomitable’ Will to Live?
articles.abstract: He spent his life trying to dodge his own mortality, resistant but haunted by its specter.
articles.snippet: He spent his life trying to dodge his own mortality, resistant but haunted by its specter.

articles.title: 2021 Was the Deadliest Year on Record for Trans Folks. Kiér Was Only 21.
articles.abstract: “She was figuring it out,” said Kiér Laprí Kartier’s best friend. “But she never got to finish figuring it out.”
articles.snippet: “She was figuring it out,” said Kiér Laprí Kartier’s best friend. “But she never got to finish figuring it out.”
